signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Intervention + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.71 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite stepped-up Japanese intervention efforts. Escalating US-Iran tensions in the Strait of Hormuz drive WTI to $104.0, increasing JPY vulnerability as an energy importer. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains crowded, suggesting intervention effects are being absorbed by carry-seekers. Carry dominates the regime while VIX remains at 17.7 (normal volatility).
signals.watch_level: 160.00 remains the key level for aggressive BoJ action; watch for VIX > 25 to trigger carry unwind.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (specs 23% long) vs RBNZ market bias signaling a hike at the next meeting on 27 May. Speculative positioning is fading the hawkish CB stance, creating elevated short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ confirms the tightening path. NZD/USD at 0.5882 remains supported by this divergence despite the 150bp carry disadvantage vs the USD.
signals.watch_level: Watch for NZD COT delta turning positive or RBNZ meeting on 27 May for confirmation.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7175 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 79% short. Institutional positioning is heavily long while retail is aggressively fading the trend. Dovish RBA tone at the 5 May meeting tempers upside, but the extreme retail short bias suggests a capitulation risk if price holds current levels. The 60bp AUD carry premium remains a structural support.
signals.watch_level: Watch for retail short percentage exceeding 85% for a contrarian reversal signal.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.731 vs 252d norm of -0.138 (z=+1.86σ). This significant decoupling indicates EUR/USD is trading as a risk-proxy rather than its historical inverse relationship with equities. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (175bp spread) structurally favors USD, keeping EUR/USD upside limited at 1.1693 despite the equity strength.
signals.watch_level: Watch for ECB meeting on 11 Jun to shift the 175bp carry deficit.
